function.name: set_system_volume
function.description: Sets the system audio master volume. Use when user asks 'set volume to 50%', 'volume max', etc.

function.name: adjust_system_volume
function.description: Increases or decreases system audio master volume by a relative amount. Use when user asks 'volume up', 'turn it down', etc.

direction.description: Whether to increase or decrease volume

function.name: toggle_system_mute
function.description: Mutes, unmutes, or toggles system audio.

function.name: set_system_brightness
function.description: Sets the system screen brightness. Use when user asks 'set brightness to 50%', 'screen is too bright', etc.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create a new text or JSON file, or overwrite an existing one. Use when user says 'write this down in notes.txt', 'save this JSON data', 'create a file called...'.

filename.description: Name of the file (e.g. notes.txt, data.json)

content.description: The text or JSON content to write to the file

as_json.description: If true, attempts to parse and save the content as a perfectly formatted JSON file. Default is false.

function.name: append_to_file
function.description: Add new text to the end of an existing file. Use when user says 'add this to my notes', 'append to grocery.txt'.

filename.description: Name of the file to append to

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read the contents of a saved file. Use when user says 'what did I write in notes.txt', 'read data.json'.

filename.description: Name of the file to read

function.name: list_files
function.description: List all files currently saved by the assistant. Use when user asks 'what files do you have saved?' or 'list my documents'.

function.name: read_pdf
function.description: Extracts text from a PDF file. Intelligently searches the user's ARIA_Files and Downloads folders automatically. Use when user asks to 'read this PDF', 'summarize the document', or 'extract text from...'.

filename.description: Name of the PDF file (e.g. math_homework.pdf or just math_homework)

page_start.description: The first page to read (1-indexed). Default is 1.

page_end.description: The last page to read (inclusive). If omitted, reads to the end.

function.name: adjust_system_brightness
function.description: Increases or decreases screen brightness by a relative amount. Use when user asks 'brightness up', 'dim the screen', etc.

direction.description: Whether to increase or decrease brightness

function.name: get_latest_news
function.description: Fetches the latest news headlines from Google News. Use when the user asks 'What's the latest news?', 'Tell me the news about AI', or 'Any news on Tesla?'.

topic.description: Optional specific topic (e.g. 'finance', 'technology', 'sports'). If omitted, fetches general top headlines.

count.description: How many headlines to read out (default is 5).

function.name: smart_media_control
function.description: Intelligently routes media commands like play, pause, next, or previous. It checks if Spotify is playing first; if not, it falls back to system media keys (which controls YouTube, Video players, etc.). Use this for ANY general media command (e.g. 'pause the video', 'next track', 'play').

function.name: open_application
function.description: Opens an application or common website by name. Examples: 'open Chrome', 'launch VS Code', 'start Discord', 'open settings'.

app_name.description: The name of the application or website to launch

function.name: minimize_window
function.description: Minimizes a specific window by its title, or the currently active window if no title is given.

title.description: Optional title of the window to minimize

function.name: maximize_window
function.description: Maximizes a specific window by its title, or the currently active window if no title is given.

title.description: Optional title of the window to maximize

function.name: close_window
function.description: Closes a specific window by its title, or the currently active window if no title is given.

title.description: Optional title of the window to close

function.name: switch_to_window
function.description: Brings a specific window to the foreground and focuses it. Examples: 'switch to VS Code', 'go to Chrome'.

title.description: Title or partial title of the window to switch to

function.name: show_desktop
function.description: Minimizes all windows to show the desktop. Example: 'show desktop', 'hide all windows'.

function.name: get_important_unread_emails
function.description: Checks the user's Gmail inbox for new, important unread emails. Uses Gmail's AI importance markers to filter out newsletters and spam. Use when user asks 'Do I have any important emails?', 'Check my mail', or 'Any new messages in my inbox?'.

max_results.description: Maximum number of emails to retrieve (default is 5)

function.name: read_specific_email
function.description: Reads the full content of a specific email by its ID. Use this after get_important_unread_emails if the user asks you to read a specific email out loud.

email_id.description: The unique ID of the email to read (provided by get_important_unread_emails)

function.name: take_screenshot
function.description: Captures a screenshot of the entire screen. Use this when the user asks what's on their screen, to see their screen, or to help with something visible.

function.name: get_clipboard
function.description: Get the current clipboard content. Use when user says 'what did I copy', 'what's in my clipboard', or 'summarize what I copied'.

function.name: set_clipboard
function.description: Copy text to the clipboard. Use when user says 'copy this', 'save to clipboard'.

text.description: The text to copy to clipboard

function.name: get_active_window
function.description: Get the title and app name of the currently active window. Use when user asks about their current app.

function.name: scrape_webpage
function.description: Extract text content from a webpage URL. Use when user wants to summarize an article or read web content.

url.description: The URL of the webpage to scrape

function.name: spotify_play
function.description: Play a song, artist, playlist, or album on Spotify. Use when user asks to play music.

query.description: Search query for content to play

function.name: spotify_pause
function.description: Pause Spotify playback.

function.name: spotify_skip_next
function.description: Skip to next track.

function.name: spotify_skip_previous
function.description: Go back to previous track.

function.name: spotify_add_to_queue
function.description: Add a song to queue.

function.name: spotify_current_track
function.description: Get the name, artist, and album of the currently playing track. Use when user asks 'what song is this', 'what's playing', 'song name', 'current song', or 'what am I listening to'.

function.name: spotify_shuffle
function.description: Enable or disable shuffle.

state.description: True for on, False for off

function.name: spotify_repeat
function.description: Set repeat mode.

function.name: spotify_volume
function.description: Set volume level.

function.name: spotify_like_current
function.description: Like current song.

function.name: spotify_unlike_current
function.description: Unlike current song.

function.name: setup_whatsapp
function.description: Setup WhatsApp auto-reply integration for the FIRST TIME. Shows QR code for authentication. ONLY use if user explicitly asks to 'setup WhatsApp', 'connect WhatsApp', or 'link WhatsApp'. Do NOT use for sending messages.

function.name: setup_discord
function.description: Setup Discord auto-reply integration. Guides user through getting Discord token. Use when user asks to setup Discord, connect Discord, or enable Discord messaging.

function.name: start_messaging
function.description: Start the messaging system (WhatsApp and/or Discord). Use when user asks to start messaging, activate auto-reply, or turn on message responses.

function.name: stop_messaging
function.description: Stop the messaging system. Use when user asks to stop messaging, deactivate auto-reply, or turn off message responses.

function.name: messaging_status
function.description: Check messaging system status and statistics. Use when user asks about messaging status, how many messages sent, or if messaging is active.

function.name: add_messaging_contact
function.description: Add a contact to the messaging whitelist. Use when user wants to add someone to auto-reply list. IMPORTANT: only use the two parameters below â€” 'platform' and 'contact'. Do NOT invent or add extra parameters like 'contact_name' or 'name'.

contact.description: Phone number for WhatsApp or Discord user ID for Discord. If the user also gives a name/label for the contact, just use the ID/number here â€” names are stored separately.

function.name: manage_whitelist
function.description: Manage messaging whitelist with natural language. Use for: listing contacts, removing contacts, clearing whitelist. Examples: 'show whitelist', 'remove John from WhatsApp', 'clear Discord whitelist', 'who's on the list'.

action.description: Action to perform: add, remove, list, or clear

platform.description: Platform (required for add/remove/clear, optional for list)

function.name: send_message
function.description: Send a message to a contact on WhatsApp or Discord. Use when user wants to send a message like 'send hey to John', 'message Mom on WhatsApp', 'text Anas hello'.

function.name: get_last_message
function.description: Get the last message received from a contact on WhatsApp or Discord. Use when user asks 'what did they say', 'read last message from X', 'show messages from Y', 'what was the last message'.

contact.description: Contact name or number (optional - if not provided, returns last message from anyone)

function.name: set_autonomous_mode
function.description: Enable or disable ARIA's autonomous messaging mode for proactive check-ins.

checkin_threshold_hours.description: Hours of silence before ARIA checks in (default 24)

function.name: send_proactive_message
function.description: Proactively message a whitelisted contact with a specific goal (e.g., a reminder).

contact_id.description: The ID or exact name of the contact

context.description: The reason or goal for the message

function.name: store_user_message
function.description: Store a message for the user when a contact asks you to tell them something or leave a message while they are away.

contact_name.description: Name of the person leaving the message

platform.description: The platform the message was received on

message_content.description: The content of the message to relay to the user

function.name: get_pending_messages
function.description: Retrieve and optionally clear pending messages left for the user.

clear.description: Whether to clear the messages after retrieving them (default: true)

function.name: set_current_status
function.description: Update the user's current status so the AI can inform contacts.

status.description: The status description (e.g. 'Aze is on a coffee break')
